page.title: Help
page.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Cycling Zone Manager

intro.title: What is Cycling Zone Manager?
intro.text: Cycling Zone Manager is a fantasy cycling manager game where you build and lead a cycling team. You buy riders at auction, negotiate transfers with other managers, meet the board's targets and compete to climb through the divisions.

firstSteps.title: First steps
steps: Create an account and log in.
Pick a team name and a manager name in the welcome wizard. Both fields are required before you can continue.
The welcome modal opens automatically the first time and walks you through the three core features: Market, Auctions and Board. It links directly to Help & Rules.
You start in Division 3 with a starting budget of {startingBalance} CZ$.
Go to Riders and find free riders you want to bid on.
Start an auction on a rider. If you win the auction, the rider is yours.
You begin with a {initialSquad}-rider starting squad. No minimum squad size is enforced, but you need riders on your roster to field a team in races.

whatBoard.title: What does the board do?
whatBoard.text: The board sets targets for your team and follows your season live: satisfaction now updates after every race weekend (it moves at most 5 points per weekend) towards what a full season evaluation would give. Satisfaction determines your sponsor modifier, i.e. how much of the sponsor income gets paid out, and the modifier follows satisfaction live. Hard consequences (salary cap, signing restriction, forced sale) are only evaluated at two predictable checkpoints: mid-season and season end. You cannot be fired; consequences are graduated and always predictable. You can follow this race by race in the plan details on the Board page, where a weekend-by-weekend history shows whether satisfaction went up or down and which goal area drove it.

season1Baseline.title: Your board is open from day one
season1Baseline.text: The board of directors is unlocked from the start of your first season, so you can pick your Club DNA and negotiate your plans right away rather than waiting a season. Your sponsor modifier stays at 1.0× until you sign your first plan, so the season-1 sponsor is neutral while you set up. The board also reads your team (national core, U25 share, specialisation and star profile) and saves a permanent identity snapshot that feeds your 5-year plan suggestions.

season2Onboarding.title: Sequential board onboarding
steps: From the start of season 1 you pick Club DNA first. Then the board opens sequentially: 5-year plan, 3-year plan, 1-year plan.
Pick a focus direction (Balanced, Youth Development, Star Signing) and negotiate each target once in the wizard.
Your Club DNA is required before the first plan: it colours both target suggestions and the board composition.

If you forget to pick DNA and negotiate before race day 5, the system picks the best-matching DNA suggestion, assigns the board and signs a plan with default focus derived from your identity snapshot.
After that the 1-year plan renews every year, the 3-year plan every 3rd year and the 5-year plan every 5th year. Never all three at once again.

strategicDashboard.title: The strategic dashboard
strategicDashboard.text: The Board page shows the three plans side by side (desktop) or stacked (mobile). Each panel shows satisfaction %, sponsor× modifier, progress per target and status icons (✓ met · ! at risk · ~ close · ○ neutral). Click a target to open a mini dialog with full details, a cumulative progress bar and a reaction from the board member who owns that category.

namedMembers.title: Named board members
namedMembers.text: Your team has 5 named board members from a pool of 9 archetypes: The Sponsor Pleaser, The Traditionalist, The Talent Scout, The Result Hunter, The Pragmatist, The Youth Idealist, The Nationalist Purist, The Classics Purist and The GC Lover. They are assigned only after Club DNA is chosen, so three match your team's season 1 identity and chosen DNA, while two wildcards bring contrast. The member with the highest alignment is chairman (★) and speaks when in doubt. Two consecutive plan expirations below 30% satisfaction replace the chairman.

clubDna.title: Club DNA
clubDna.text: When your team is created you pick one of 5 club DNA archetypes before the board is assembled: Scandinavian development team, Italian classics traditionalist, Sprint-focused commercial, French climber heritage, British all-rounder. DNA affects three things: (1) 5-year plans get an extra tradition target (e.g. a podium in a classic, incl. Monuments, for italian_classics), (2) targets matching DNA priorities get boosted satisfaction bonus and penalty, (3) board members and future chairman swaps tip towards matching archetypes. You can still change your DNA until your first season ends; after that it's locked in. Switching re-assigns your board members and target weighting.

consequenceTiers.title: Consequence tiers (6 layers)
rows: Satisfaction <40% for 2 evaluations in a row
Salary cap: total salary capped at 1.5x its level when triggered, blocking buys/contract extensions that would grow it further. Never triggers in a team's first 30 days
Forced sale: your lowest-value rider is auto-listed (riders with popularity ≥70 or value ≥8,000,000 CZ$ are protected)
Sponsor pullout: −10% sponsor for one season
Bonus offer: +200K for one extra target

requestsAndLocks.title: Board requests and pivot locks
requestsAndLocks.text: Once per season you can send a board request to pivot your plan (e.g. 'more star focus'). The reply is approved, partial, rejected or approved with a tradeoff. Major pivots (youth/star) are only allowed once per plan lifecycle. 5-year and 3-year plans cannot be pivoted in the first 50% of their runtime unless satisfaction has changed by more than 30 points. In the last 5 race days of a season all requests are blocked.

midSeasonCheck.title: Mid-season check
midSeasonCheck.text: Midway through the season (half of race days completed) the board automatically checks your plan status. If satisfaction is below 50% or at least half of measurable targets are 'behind', you get an 'Action required' notification in your inbox. The mid-season point is also the first hard checkpoint: if satisfaction has dropped below the consequence thresholds by then, the board applies the matching consequence layers (the second checkpoint is season end). Between checkpoints satisfaction moves freely without triggering hard consequences.

whatAuctions.title: What are auctions?
whatAuctions.text: Auctions are primarily used to buy free riders (riders without a team). You list a rider for auction, all managers can bid, and the highest bid wins. You can also list your own riders for auction. If you start an auction on a free or AI-owned rider, you are only the auction initiator. You don't receive the sale proceeds unless the rider was actually on your team.

howToStart.title: How to start an auction
steps: Go to Riders and find a free or AI rider.
Click the rider's name to open the stats page.
Click 'Start auction' and set a starting price. Minimum equals the rider's Value.
On free and AI riders the starting price is your first bid, so you lead the auction immediately.
The auction is now visible to everyone under Auctions.

bidding.title: Bidding on auctions
bidding.text: Go to Auctions and find an auction you want to bid on. Enter your bid and click 'Bid'. You can see whether you're winning (Winning) or whether you've bid but been outbid (You bid). The minimum bid is 1 CZ$ above the current price (or matching the asking price if no one has bid yet).

valuation.title: The valuation next to a bid
valuation.text: Each auction shows the rider's estimated market value, and the percentage tells you how far the current bid sits below or above it. The valuation is the game's model estimate meant as a reference to help you judge a bid, not a guaranteed fair price.

autoBidCap.title: Auto-bid cap
autoBidCap.text: Auto-bid cap lets you set your private maximum. If you're not already leading, the auto-bid places the minimum bid for you straight away so you take the lead. After that it bids for you automatically each time someone outbids you, raising your bid 1 CZ$ at a time and never above your cap. You stay in front until another manager goes higher than your cap, and only then are you outbid. Your cap is private, so other managers never see it. If your balance can no longer cover your cap, the auto-bid stops until you free up funds.

tieRule.title: Ties on equal bids
tieRule.text: If another manager bids exactly your auto-bid cap, you keep the lead at that same price with no increase. When two bids match, the manager who reached that amount first holds the lead. Setting a cap therefore also defends your lead against anyone who only matches it.

whenExpires.title: When does an auction expire?
whenExpires.text: An auction runs for 1 active hour. Night hours don't count: the active window is 08:00 to 24:00 every day, and trading pauses 00:00 to 08:00. If you bid at 19:40, the auction ends the same evening at 20:40. If you bid at 23:30, the remaining time carries over the night and the auction ends the next morning at 08:30.

flashAuction.title: Flash Auction (Deadline Day)
flashAuction.text: During Deadline Day you can choose Flash Auction when starting an auction. A Flash Auction lasts exactly 30 minutes from the start time, regardless of active windows. The option only shows up on the rider's stats page when Deadline Day is active.

tenMinExtend.title: 10-minute extension
tenMinExtend.text: If a bid is placed within the last 10 minutes of an auction, the auction is automatically extended by 10 minutes from the bid time. This continues until no bid is placed in the final 10 minutes. The extension cannot spill past the day's active window: if the extra 10 minutes would land after close, the auction skips straight to the next window's opening instead, carrying over only the leftover minutes (e.g. a bid that would have ended the auction 5 minutes after close instead ends it 5 minutes into the next window).

squadAndAuctions.title: Squad size and auctions
squadAndAuctions.text: Squad limits still apply per division. Active auctions where you lead reserve both balance and a potential squad slot, so you can't lead more auctions than your squad can hold.

whatTransfers.title: What is the transfer system?
whatTransfers.text: The transfer system lets you negotiate directly with other managers to buy or sell riders, like in Football Manager. You can send an offer on any rider, whether listed for sale or not.

sellOnTransferList.title: Sell via the transfer list
sellOnTransferList.text: You can list your own riders for sale at a fixed asking price, either from My Squad or directly from the rider's profile page. While a rider is listed, you can edit the asking price or remove the listing at any time, both from the Transfers page and from the rider's profile. Buyers can sort the market by price or by newest listings.

sendOffer.title: Send an offer
steps: Find the rider you want to buy under Riders and click his name.
Click 'Send transfer offer' at the bottom of the page.
Enter your offer in CZ$ and an optional message.
Click 'Send offer'. The seller gets a notification.
AI riders cannot receive direct offers; they must be bought via auction.

proposeSwap.title: Propose a rider swap
steps: Find the rider you want in a swap and go to their profile page.
Click '⇄ Propose swap', pick one of your own riders to offer, and optionally add a cash adjustment.
Click 'Send swap'. The other manager gets a notification.
Existing swap proposals still show up under Received offers and Sent offers on the Transfers page, where you can accept, counter or withdraw them.

receiveAndReply.title: Receive and reply to offers
receiveAndReply.text: Go to Transfers, Received offers. Here you see all offers on your riders. You can accept, reject or send a counter-offer with your own price.

finalConfirmation.title: Final confirmation
finalConfirmation.text: When an offer or counter-offer is accepted, the deal enters a short confirmation phase. Both managers must confirm the final agreement, and the system re-checks ownership, balance and squad limits just before the deal goes through.

midRaceTransfers.title: Trades during an active stage race
midRaceTransfers.text: If a rider is traded (transfer, swap or auction) while he is mid-way through an active stage race, the deal and payment complete immediately, but he finishes the race for his selling team and joins his new team right after the finish. His profile shows the upcoming team in the meantime, and he cannot be traded again until the move completes. Races that have not started yet are unaffected: the rider moves instantly.

negotiation.title: Back-and-forth negotiation
negotiation.text: Negotiation can continue indefinitely. The seller sends a counter, the buyer can accept the counter, send a new offer or withdraw. The round counter shows how far along you are in the negotiation.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.text: Offers are private. Only you and the seller can see your negotiation. Other managers cannot see who has bid on the same rider.

archiveCompleted.title: Archive completed offers
archiveCompleted.text: Offers that are rejected, withdrawn or otherwise completed can be archived from the Transfers page. Archiving removes them from active lists, so ongoing negotiations are always easy to find.

profile.title: Manager profile
profile.text: Every manager has a public profile page. Click a team name in the leaderboard, team list or transfers to view their profile. The profile shows team and riders, season history, achievements and transfer activity.

namesAndInit.title: Team name and manager name
namesAndInit.text: On My Profile you can save or edit both team name and manager name. If your account was previously created without a real team, you can also initialise the team from here by saving the team info.

accountSettings.title: Change username or email
accountSettings.text: Under My Profile, Account you can change your username and your email. A username is 3-20 characters (letters, numbers, underscore or hyphen) and must be unique. Changing your email sends a confirmation link to the new address; the change takes effect only after you click that link, so your login keeps working until then.

theme.title: Theme (light / dark)
theme.text: Under Settings, Appearance you can pick between 'Follow system' (auto based on your device), 'Light' and 'Dark'. The choice is saved locally in your browser. The sidebar stays dark in both themes for consistency.

forgotPassword.title: Forgot password
forgotPassword.text: If you can't log in, you can pick 'Forgot password?' from the login page. The system sends a reset link to your email, and the link opens a dedicated page where you can pick a new password.

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.text: The inbox (under Clubhouse) collects all system events: bids, auctions, transfer offers, season start, economy and achievements. Click a message to jump directly to the relevant page. Unread messages show as a yellow count next to the bell icon.

onlineStatus.title: Online status
onlineStatus.text: A green dot next to a manager's name means they are active right now (last seen within 5 minutes). A grey dot shows they are currently offline. You can see this on manager profiles and next to each team in League & standings.

managersOnline.title: Managers online count
managersOnline.text: On the Dashboard you can see how many managers are active in the game right now. This updates automatically.

headToHead.title: Compare two teams (Head-to-Head)
headToHead.text: Open League & standings (under Season & Results). Click the rank number on two teams to select them, then press Compare to open a side-by-side drawer: season points, stage and GC wins, transfer history between the teams and both teams' current top-5 riders. Your own team is selected first by default.

whyDms.title: Why Discord DMs?
whyDms.text: Cycling Zone sends notifications (outbids, won auctions, transfer offers and replies) as private messages directly to you on Discord, so you don't need the app open to catch a time-sensitive situation. It doesn't replace in-app notifications; it complements them.

howToGetDms.title: How to get DMs
steps: In Discord: Settings, Advanced, enable Developer Mode.
Right-click your own name anywhere in Discord and pick 'Copy User ID'.
In Cycling Zone: go to Profile, Discord Integration and paste your ID in the field. Click 'Save Discord ID'.
Make sure you share a server with the Cycling Zone bot, and that 'Allow direct messages from server members' is enabled on that server.
Click 'Send test DM' to verify it works.

optOut.title: Turn DMs off (opt-out)
optOut.text: If you don't want private messages from the bot, you can turn off 'Receive DMs for personal events' under Profile, Discord Integration. In-app notifications keep working. Personal info (outbids, won auctions, transfer offers and replies) is not posted to the shared channel; that channel is reserved for broadcasts.

whenDmsSent.title: When are DMs sent?
rows: Your transfer offer is accepted / rejected / countered
A new auction is created (all managers)

whatAchievements.title: What are achievements?
whatAchievements.text: Achievements are rewards you unlock by reaching specific milestones in the game. There are 45 achievements across 5 categories: Auctions, Transfers, Team, Season and Secret.

categories.title: Categories
rows: First bid, Sniper, High Roller, 50 won auctions
Full squad, Youth team, Star team, Promoted!
Division winner, Podium, Board favourite, Veteran
Unlocked by unexpected events, not described upfront

secretAchievements.title: Secret achievements
secretAchievements.text: Secret achievements show as locked with the title '???' until you unlock them. The description is only revealed afterwards. They unlock when you do unexpected or fun things in the game.

notifications.title: Achievement notifications
notifications.text: When you unlock an achievement, you get a message in the inbox with the achievement icon and title. Your unlocked achievements show on your manager profile.

whatWatchlist.title: What is the Watchlist?
whatWatchlist.text: The Watchlist is your private list of riders you're tracking. The list is only visible to you.

howToAdd.title: How to add a rider
steps: Find a rider in the Rider Database or on a rider's stats page.
Click the ☆ star next to the rider's name.
The star turns yellow (★) and the rider is now on your list.
Go to Watchlist in the menu to see your full list.

saleNotification.title: Sale notification
saleNotification.text: If a rider on your watchlist is listed for sale via the transfer list or put up for auction, you automatically get a notification in your inbox. You stay updated on your favourites without actively checking the market.

watchlistCounter.title: Watchlist counter
watchlistCounter.text: On a rider's stats page you can see 'X managers are tracking this rider'. This shows the total number of managers who have the rider on their watchlist, without revealing who. Use this as a demand signal.

features.title: Watchlist features
features.text: You can sort and filter your saved riders on all stats, Value, salary, age, U25/U23 and free agent. You can add private notes to each rider. On free agents you can start an auction directly from the watchlist.

whatActivity.title: What is My Activity?
whatActivity.text: My Activity is your personal market workspace under Market. It gathers all your market actions (auctions, transfers and watchlist) in one place, separate from the global inbox.

tabs.title: The tabs
steps: Action required: Offers awaiting your reply, counters you've received, deals awaiting your confirmation and auctions ending within 1 hour. Opens by default.
Auctions: All active auctions where you're seller or bidder.
Transfers: Offers you've received and offers you've sent that are still active.
Watchlist: Compact view of your saved riders with market status. Click → for the full watchlist.
History: Completed auctions and closed transfers.

deepLinks.title: Deep links
deepLinks.text: Clicking a row in My Activity takes you directly to the relevant page: auctions, transfers or the rider's stats page. Clicking the rider name within a row opens the rider's stats page.

seasonFlow.title: Season flow
steps: Admin closes the transfer window and starts a new season.

At season start: pending transfers are processed, sponsor money is paid out first, then loan interest and salaries are deducted (each salary is frozen at signing and does not follow the rider's later market value). An emergency loan is created only if sponsor + balance can't cover the shortfall.
Races are run by the Cycling Zone race engine, which simulates each stage and writes the results automatically.

At season end: points are tallied, division bonus is paid out, promotion/relegation is decided (every season), and board plans are evaluated. Salaries and interest are no longer deducted at season end. They moved to the next season's start (v3.78).

racesAndResults.title: Races and results
racesAndResults.text: Races are run by the Cycling Zone race engine. It simulates each stage from rider abilities, form and tactics, then writes the standings and prize money automatically. Prize money lands on your team balance as soon as a race is finalised. You can follow every result under Season & Results, Races. While a stage race is in progress, the race page also shows the full running standings after each completed stage: GC, points, mountains, youth and team. The final GC is always the sum of the published stage gaps, so you can verify it yourself.

latestTeamResult.title: Where do I see how my team did?
latestTeamResult.text: After a race with your riders is finalized, your dashboard shows a 'How your team did' card with your placements, prize money and a short recap of the race. Follow the link on the card to the full result with every stage and classification.

stageProfile.title: Terrain on a result
stageProfile.text: When you open a result, each stage shows a small terrain badge with a schematic silhouette (flat, hilly, mountain, time trial and so on) plus the likely finale. This is an indicative terrain category based on the stage's difficulty, not a measured elevation profile with distance and climbing metres.

raceDetail.title: Open a race: route, terrain DNA and your lineup
raceDetail.text: Race names are clickable across the game. Opening a race shows its results if it has run, or for an upcoming race the route profile per stage, a terrain-DNA bar of which abilities each stage rewards, and your own lineup with a route match per stage so you can pick riders that suit the parcours. Pick a stage from the stage stripe to switch the profile and the route match. The terrain is an indicative category, not a measured elevation profile. On a stage's result tab you can switch between Stage, Overall, Points, Mountain, Youth and Teams to see the standings after that stage, and a team filter lets you narrow the tables to all teams, your own team, or one specific team.

teamSelection.title: Pick your team and move riders between races
teamSelection.text: For each race you enter you field a full team: pick every rider slot the race has. If you would rather sit a race out, withdraw from it; if you are short on riders, sign free agents from the Transfers page. When two of your races overlap on the same day, a rider can only be in one of them. On the Squad distribution board you can move a rider from one race to another by dragging the rider on desktop, or by tapping the rider and choosing the other race on phones. The race you move them out of shows as understaffed until you fill it again, and the assistant's Auto-fill does that quickly.

prizeMoney.title: Prize money from races
prizeMoney.text: Each result for a rider on your team generates prize money: points for the placing × {prizePerPoint} CZ$. Prize money is paid automatically to your team balance when admin finalises the results and shows as a 'prize' transaction in your finance log. Points for the season leaderboard and prize money are computed separately: points decide the leaderboard, while the CZ$ prize only affects your balance. Races without a race class generate no prize money. See the full points table under Season & Results, Races, Points & prizes.

raceLibrary.title: Race library
raceLibrary.text: Under Season & Results, Races, Library you can search and filter all races across seasons (season, class, status, name). The list is sorted newest-first, and a 'My division only' filter narrows it to races in your own division so you can quickly find what you raced today. Click a race to open its history page with previous editions, the winner of each season and a cumulative overview of the riders who have historically performed best in that race.

promotionRelegation.title: Promotion and relegation
promotionRelegation.text: The divisions form a pyramid of pools: 1 pool in Division 1, 2 in Division 2, 4 in Division 3 and 8 in Division 4. At season end each pool promotes its top 2 teams up to the pool above, and relegates its bottom 4 teams down, split evenly between the two pools below it. Division 1 does not promote (it is the top) and the bottom division does not relegate. It is decided automatically from your pool's final standings, every season. Only real managers move between pools; AI teams are filler and are topped up afterwards. Division 4 opens gradually as its pools fill with real managers, so a Division 3 pool's relegation starts once its Division 4 pools open.

whenSeasonEnds.title: When can a season be ended?
whenSeasonEnds.text: Admin can only end a season once all pending race results for the season's races have been processed. As long as submissions are waiting for approval or rejection, season completion is blocked.

adminRecomputeStandings.title: Admin: recompute standings on drift
adminRecomputeStandings.text: If an active or completed season is missing standings due to older data drift, admin can use the '↻ Standings' button on the Admin page. It recomputes the table from the stored `race_results`, creates the missing standings rows and updates the division's internal rank that the board uses at season end.

adminBetaReset.title: Admin: beta reset
adminBetaReset.text: Under Admin, Beta test tools admin can run a partial reset or a full test reset. The suite can cancel open market activity, return manager riders to the AI/free pool, reset balances, divisions, boards, race calendar, seasons, XP/level and achievement unlocks. AI teams, bank teams and frozen teams are not affected by the manager resets.

formula.title: The prize formula
formula.text: Prize money is computed directly from points: 1 point = {prizePerPoint} CZ$. Only races with a race class (Grand Tour, Major Tour, Monuments, WorldTour, ProSeries, Class 1, Class 2) generate prize money. Races without a class give 0 CZ$.

payout.title: Payout
payout.text: Prize money is paid automatically to your balance when admin finalises race results and shows as a 'prize' transaction in your finance log. Points for the season leaderboard and prize money are computed separately: points decide the leaderboard, while the CZ$ prize only affects your balance.

fullTable.title: The full points table
fullTable.text: Under Season & Results, Races, Points & prizes you'll find every race class's complete points and prize scale with all places.
fullTable.disclaimer: Prize amounts may still be adjusted during the open beta.

overview.title: Division overview
overview.text: The game has 4 divisions. Division 1 is the highest. Everyone starts in Division 3.

promotionRelegation.title: Promotion and relegation
promotionRelegation.text: Each pool promotes its top 2 up to the pool above and relegates its bottom 4 down, split between the two pools below. Division 1 does not promote and the bottom division does not relegate. Decided automatically from your pool's standings, every season.

valueAndPrice.title: Value and price
valueAndPrice.text: A rider's market value is their base value (calculated from the rider's stats and rider type) plus the average of the rider's prize money over the most recent seasons (up to 3). The starting price on an auction must at minimum equal the rider's Value.

salary.title: Salary
salary.text: Each rider's annual salary is set at signing as about 6.7% of their market value at that moment, and is then frozen: it does not change automatically as the rider develops. The salary is visible as a separate column in the Rider Database and on the watchlist. Salaries are paid automatically at the start of the next season, right after sponsor money lands. If sponsor + your existing balance can't cover the salaries, the system automatically takes an emergency loan.

abilitiesExplained.title: The 15 abilities and where they matter
rows: Explosive kick to launch or close a gap

development.title: Development over time
development.text: On a rider's stats page there's a 'Development' tab. Here you can pick an ability, follow its development as a graph, and see recent historical data points. New data points are added as riders develop, for example at season changes. If the tab is empty, the rider has no recorded development yet.

trainingFocus.title: Training focus
trainingFocus.text: From the profile of one of your own riders you can set a season-long training focus: a direction (VO2max, Threshold/TT, Sprint, Endurance, Technique or Aero) plus an intensity. The trained abilities then develop faster toward their ceiling, applied at the next season change. Higher intensity trains faster but carries a setback risk (lost gains): Easy is safe, Normal has a small risk, Hard a clear one. Other abilities grow a little slower while a focus is active. You have 3 focus slots per season, so pick your key riders; changing an existing focus costs nothing. Training is free and does not cost money.

scouting.title: Scouting potential
scouting.text: A rider's potential is hidden until you scout them: unscouted riders on other teams show no estimate at all. Every team has a talent scout, a real staff member if you have hired one, otherwise a default scout, who takes on jobs from the Scouting page or a rider's profile. A targeted job investigates one rider and reports back in a few days; a mission covers a division, a country, U23 riders or a national championship for a shortlist of prospects, plus one free report on the best find. Jobs cost travel money and take real time, and your scout can only run one or two jobs at once depending on his rating. A better scout narrows his estimates further, but no scout ever knows a rider exactly: even fully scouted riders, and your own squad, show a narrow range rather than an exact star value. Estimates are personal to your team, so two managers can read the same talent differently, and race results are what settle who was right. The Scouting tab on the rider profile collects the full report: the verdict, potential per rider type with a ceiling band, and a value comparison. Potential is always shown as stars and a verdict, never as a number.

u25u23.title: U25 and U23 riders
u25u23.text: Riders under 25 and 23 years old are tagged. You can filter on U25/U23 in the rider database and watchlist.

whatDailyTraining.title: What is Daily Training?
whatDailyTraining.text: Daily Training lets you develop your riders every day instead of only at season transitions. On the Daily Training page you set a program for each rider (a focus direction and an intensity), and the system applies it automatically each day. Daily Training is active, live since the season 1 relaunch.

programs.title: Setting programs
steps: Go to Daily Training in the navigation.
Find a rider and choose a focus: VO2max, Threshold, Sprint, Endurance, Technique or Aero.
Choose an intensity: Rest, Easy, Normal or Hard.
Save. The program runs every day until you change it.
There is no cap on how many riders can have a program: you can set one for your entire squad.

focusAbilities.title: What each focus trains
focusAbilities.text: Each focus develops a specific set of abilities faster toward their ceiling, while a rider's other abilities keep improving a little more slowly. Everyone develops across the board, just fastest in the chosen direction. The directions are: VO2max trains Climbing, Punch and Tempo. Threshold/TT trains Time trial and Tempo. Sprint trains Sprint and Acceleration. Endurance trains Endurance, Recovery and Durability. Technique trains Descending, Positioning and Cobbles. Aero trains Time trial and Flat. Intensity sets the pace: Easy is slowest but safe, Normal is the balanced default with a small setback risk, and Hard is fastest but risks a setback or injury when a rider's fatigue is already high.

typeGating.title: Why doesn't a focus grow for some riders?
typeGating.text: Every rider has a type (Sprinter, Climber, TT, GC and so on) and a hidden potential per ability, both set when the rider is created. A training focus only speeds up abilities that suit the rider's type: a Climber's Sprint and Acceleration barely move under a Sprint focus, no matter the intensity, because that type has little natural room to grow there. Check a rider's type badge before picking a focus, and the focus dropdown marks each option so you can see at a glance whether it suits the rider, is limited, or won't develop at all.

trainToday.title: Train today and the +25% boost
trainToday.text: Click 'Train today' once per day to run all active programs with a +25% training boost. If you skip the button, the assistant runs training automatically after 22:00. You never lose a day of training. Only the boost is missed when you don't click.

smartDefault.title: What trains a rider with no focus set?
smartDefault.text: A rider with no focus chosen is not left untrained: the assistant trains them toward a focus that suits their type, for example a Sprinter defaults to Sprint and a Climber to VO2max. You'll see a small 'Assistant: ... (auto)' note under the focus dropdown when this applies. Setting your own focus always overrides it. To apply a type-matched focus to several riders at once, select them and choose 'Smart focus (by rider type)' in the bulk-apply bar; riders who already have a focus set are skipped so your existing choices are never overwritten.

formFatigue.title: Form and Fatigue
formFatigue.text: Every rider has a Form value and a Fatigue value, both on a 0-100 scale, visible on the rider profile. Training and racing build Fatigue. Every day around midnight each rider also recovers some Fatigue, so it never gets stuck at 100: rest and easy days recover the most, normal training settles at a sustainable level, and hard training still builds it up over the day. A high Recovery rating speeds daily recovery up. Form follows the balance between the two: high Fatigue keeps Form low, while a well-rested rider's Form rises. Both values update daily. In races (active since the season 1 relaunch), form gives a small scoring edge and fatigue a small penalty, capped at about ±3% so favorites still win on ability. Race days build fatigue based on stage profile: mountain stages cost the most, flat stages the least. Injured riders are not entered into races automatically.

injuryRisk.title: Injury risk
injuryRisk.text: Running Hard intensity training on a rider who already has high Fatigue triggers an injury risk. A risk badge appears on the rider before training runs. If the roll is unlucky, the rider picks up a mild injury and is out for 1-5 days. Dropping intensity to Normal or Rest removes the risk.

progressBars.title: Progress bars and ability points
progressBars.text: Rider profiles show a progress bar for each ability being trained. The bar fills as training accumulates. When the bar reaches full, the rider gains +1 in that ability. Progress carries over day to day. Nothing resets mid-season.

longTermGrowth.title: Long-term growth
longTermGrowth.text: Daily Training fits within the same long-term growth model as season transitions: young riders peak around age 27-28 and then gradually decline. Daily training accelerates progress toward each ability's ceiling but cannot push a rider beyond their potential.

readingReport.title: Reading the training report
readingReport.text: After training runs, the report opens with a day summary: how many riders trained, how many had a breakthrough (an ability rose +1), and how many are in peak form. Each row shows the focus ability's progress toward its next +1, and a breakthrough is highlighted with the actual jump, for example 71 to 72. The roster table shows the same progress bar, so you can steer a focus toward a rider who is close to a breakthrough.

trainingHistory.title: Training history
trainingHistory.text: Every daily training run is kept. The Daily Training page lists your last 30 days below the roster, each day showing how many riders trained, breakthroughs and riders in peak form; expand a day to see the full per-rider report. Each rider's profile has its own training history under the Development tab, listing that rider's focus, intensity, gains and form day by day.

weeklyRhythm.title: Weekly rhythm
weeklyRhythm.text: The 'Weekly rhythm' panel on the Training page lets you set a target intensity for each day of the week (Monday to Sunday) for your whole team at once. It steers intensity only, never focus: each rider keeps training their own chosen focus (or the assistant's type-matched default). A day in the rhythm overrides that rider's season intensity for that day only; a day left unset falls back to the rider's own program. Teams that never set a rhythm are unaffected, riders simply keep training at their season intensity every day like before. The 'Train today' consistency bonus (+25%) still only applies when you click it yourself; the rhythm does not grant it automatically.

individualWeeklyPlan.title: Individual weekly plan per rider
individualWeeklyPlan.text: Click 'Individual weekly plan' on any rider's row in the roster to set that rider's own target intensity per day, separate from the team rhythm. A rider's own plan wins over the team's weekly rhythm for that rider only, which in turn wins over their season intensity. It's meant for the 2-3 riders you like to micro-manage, not the whole squad. Riders with their own plan show an 'Own plan' badge in the roster.

whatActivityFeed.title: What is the Activity feed?
whatActivityFeed.text: The Activity feed is a public stream of what's happening in the game. You can see who's winning auctions, completing transfers and when seasons start and end. The feed updates in real time.

whatShown.title: What appears in the feed?
rows: Team won an auction (shows rider and price)
A team has listed a rider for auction
A transfer between two managers was accepted
Admin has started a new season

transferRumors.title: Transfer rumours
transferRumors.text: If a manager views one of your riders, you get an anonymous notification: 'A manager is watching your rider X'. You get at most one notification per rider per hour to avoid spam.

whatContract.title: What is a rider contract?
whatContract.text: Every owned rider has a contract: a frozen salary, a length (1 to 3 seasons) and an expiry season. The salary is locked in at the moment of signing and does not change automatically as the rider develops. Free agents (riders without a team) have no contract until they are signed.

frozenSalary.title: Why salary is frozen (gold contracts and burdens)
frozenSalary.text: Because salary is locked at signing, a young rider who develops into a better player becomes a bargain: you are paying an early-career rate for a now-stronger rider. This is a 'gold contract'. The opposite is also true: a fading star signed at peak value remains on a high salary even as their ability drops, becoming a financial burden. Spotting these situations early is a core part of squad management.

contractOnTrade.title: Contracts follow the rider
contractOnTrade.text: When a rider is traded, their contract transfers unchanged to the new team. The buyer inherits the existing salary and the remaining contract length. The contract is never reset or renegotiated on trade.

freeAgents.title: Free agents
freeAgents.text: Free agents have no salary until a team signs them. The rider profile and auction page show an estimated salary (roughly 6.7% of current market value) so you can plan your budget before bidding. The actual salary is set only at the moment of signing.

whatAcademy.title: What is the Academy?
whatAcademy.text: The Academy lets you develop your own young talent instead of buying established riders on the open market. Each season your club receives a cohort of prospects at the start of the season. You review them, sign 0-2 into your Academy, and develop them in-house. The Academy is separate from your senior squad and is active, live since the season 1 relaunch.

intakeCohort.title: Season intake cohort
intakeCohort.text: At the start of each season the system generates a small cohort of prospects for your club. Prospects are teenagers, so they start with low, raw stats: judge them mainly by their potential rather than their current numbers, and scout a prospect to narrow down how far it can go. You can then decide how many (if any) to sign, and you are never forced to sign anyone.

signingProspects.title: Signing prospects
steps: Go to Academy in the navigation.
Review your intake cohort for the season.
Sign 0-2 prospects. Each signing costs a one-time signing fee and commits you to a youth salary.
Prospects you do not sign are put up for auction as youth prospects (see Youth auctions below).

academySize.title: Academy size (8 places)
academySize.text: Your Academy holds up to {academySlots} riders. These {academySlots} places are completely separate from your senior squad cap of {squadCap}. Academy riders never count toward your senior squad limit and are not eligible for races.

dailyTrainingBoost.title: Daily training and the youth boost
dailyTrainingBoost.text: Academy riders are young and start with low, raw stats, not finished pros. Their potential sets both how high their abilities can ultimately reach and how fast they improve: a bigger talent develops quicker and tops out higher, while a smaller talent grows into a solid domestique. They also get an extra youth boost while young that fades toward their mid-twenties. The same Daily Training rules apply: set a focus and intensity for each rider, and gains accumulate day by day toward their potential.

youthAuctions.title: Youth auctions
youthAuctions.text: When you reject an intake prospect, they go up for a youth auction (marked Youth) that any club can bid on. The winning club takes the prospect into its Academy, paying the winning bid and using one of its 8 places. A prospect nobody bids on leaves the sport for good, so rejecting a candidate is a real decision. No real money is ever involved, only in-game points.

upkeepCost.title: Academy upkeep
upkeepCost.text: Running the Academy costs an upkeep fee per occupied slot, charged automatically at season payroll alongside your senior salaries. Plan your budget accordingly: a full Academy of {academySlots} riders carries a noticeable running cost each season.

graduation.title: Graduating at 22
graduation.text: When an academy rider turns 22 they age out of the Academy and you make a choice for each one: promote them into your senior squad (needs a free senior place and gives them a senior contract), sell them on the transfer market, or release them as a free agent. You get a window to decide; if you do not act in time, the club picks a sensible default for you (promote when there is room, otherwise sell). This gives home-grown talent a clear path to your senior team.

promoteDemote.title: Promoting up and moving riders down
promoteDemote.text: You do not have to wait for graduation. On the Academy page you can promote any academy rider straight into your senior squad whenever you have a free senior place; they take a senior contract and the senior wage. The reverse also works: on your team page, any senior rider aged 22 or under can be moved down into your academy (as long as the academy has a free place and the rider is not on an auction or transfer list). A demoted rider drops to a youth salary, stops counting toward your senior squad, and is pulled from any upcoming races they were entered in. Both actions show the full effect before you confirm. You can also extend a contract, release a rider, or move riders between your senior squad and academy straight from a rider's own profile page.

whatFacilities.title: What are club facilities?
whatFacilities.text: Facilities are club-level upgrades you build and level up on the Club page, separate from your riders. Each facility has 5 tiers: a one-time build/upgrade price per tier, plus a smaller upkeep cost every season once built. You can also hire a chief for a facility; the chief's overall rating decides how much of the facility's effect you actually get, from about half at a bare facility with no chief up to the full effect with a top-rated chief.

tracks.title: The 5 facility tracks
rows: Speeds up your riders' daily ability growth
Unlocks hiring and upgrading your chief scout
Speeds up form and injury recovery
Boosts sponsor income (capped, a long-term investment)

liveTracksStatus.title: Only Training and Scouting are active right now
liveTracksStatus.text: Of the 5 tracks, only the Training Center and Scouting Network are wired into the game today. The other 3 show what they will do so you can plan ahead, but building or upgrading them has no in-game effect yet. Their Coming soon tag will change automatically to Active the moment their engine goes live, no action needed from you.

staffSynergy.title: How a chief and the facility work together
staffSynergy.text: A facility's tier sets its base effect. Hiring a chief for that track then decides how much of that base effect you actually get: a facility with no chief runs at roughly half strength, and a chief's overall rating scales it up toward full strength as the rating approaches the top. In short, the facility is the ceiling and the chief is how close you get to it.

hireChiefScout.title: Hiring a chief scout
hireChiefScout.text: The Scouting Network's tier sets the highest tier of chief scout you're allowed to hire, so build or upgrade it first if the candidate you want is locked. Every team already has a default scout even with no facility built, but a hired chief scout has a real, higher overall rating: that rating narrows his estimates further (a tighter potential range on the Scouting tab) and, once his overall reaches about 80, lets him run two scouting jobs at once instead of one. Hire and fire chiefs from the Club page, same as any other facility track.

costs.title: Costs: one-time build price + recurring upkeep
costs.text: Each tier has a one-time build or upgrade price, paid once when you build it. On top of that, every built tier carries a smaller upkeep cost that is charged again automatically every season, alongside any staff salaries, so budget for it as a recurring line, not just the upfront price.

what.title: Picking your race squad
what.text: Every race needs a complete lineup: the full field size for its class (6 in most races, 7 in the bigger World Tour races and Monuments, 8 in Grand Tours). Open the race page and use the Team selection panel. You can't save a partial squad. To sit a race out, withdraw from it; if you're short on healthy riders, sign free agents to fill the gaps. If you don't pick, your assistant auto-fills a full lineup from your available riders, so you never miss a race.

suitability.title: Reading the selection table
suitability.text: Each rider shows their type (such as Sprinter or Climber), a Suitability score from 0 to 100 that rates how well they fit this race's terrain, plus current Form and Fatigue. Higher suitability means a better match for the parcours. Injured riders can't be picked; use Hide injured to remove them from the list while you choose.

roles.title: Captain, sprint captain, breakaway hunter and free role
roles.text: You give each selected rider a role using tactics cards, and each card explains why the role fits this race's terrain. Your captain is protected by the team: strong, fresh helpers boost the captain's chances. On flat stages your sprint captain (if set) gets the protection instead. A breakaway hunter is always sent into the day's breakaway and rides harder to stay clear. The hunter card shows how likely a breakaway is to stay away on this terrain, and the race detail page ranks your best hunters by aggression, the urge to attack. Team work has a price: helpers lose places working for the captain, and that work is exactly what buys the captain's boost. A rider on Free role skips team duties and rides his own race, with no work cost and no captain support.

stageTactics.title: Stage races: roles and effort per stage
stageTactics.text: In stage races the race page has a stage-by-stage tactics board. Completed stages are locked and show what happened; upcoming stages can still be changed while the race runs. Each rider can ride a different role per stage, plus an effort: Work goes all in for the team today but builds extra fatigue, Normal rides normally, and Save legs eases off to stay fresher for the days that matter. If a rider takes the leader's jersey, a shortcut makes him captain for the remaining stages. Stages where you set nothing simply use the rider's race role.

strategy.title: Team strategy (standing preferences)
strategy.text: Open Team strategy from the squad board to set preferences your assistant follows when it auto-fills the calendar. Rank an A-chain of core riders. They get priority for the races you mark as targets. Give a rider a fixed role (always captain, or always sprint captain when selected). Rank captains 1/2/3 for each terrain type (flat, hills, mountains, cobbles, time trial); the assistant picks the highest-ranked rider who makes that race's lineup. Mark your target races so your strongest riders go there. Preview shows exactly how your strategy changes selections before you save, and Regenerate suggestions applies it without touching the picks you made by hand.

breakaway.title: Breakaways
breakaway.text: On flat, rolling and medium mountain stages a small breakaway gets a chance to stay away. Most get caught, but sometimes the break makes it. Aggressive riders and designated hunters join breaks more often. Stage results mark riders who were in the breakaway with a flag, showing whether they stayed away to the finish or were caught by the bunch.

fatigue.title: Fatigue matters
fatigue.text: Tired helpers support their captain less, and racing builds fatigue. Rotating your squad between races keeps your stars fresh for the days that matter.

scouting.title: Scouting other divisions
scouting.text: Switch the race hub to 'My division' or 'Other divisions' to browse any pool's upcoming start lists, read-only. Start lists open seven days before each race and show gross squads only (which riders a team has entered), so you can size up the expected field. Roles, form and fatigue stay hidden, and races further out stay locked until their start list opens.

seasonPlanner.a: The Season Planner is where you plan form peaks for your whole squad. Target a key race for a rider and the planner builds a lead-up that lifts their form for that day. Form is earned through training in the lead-up, so a peak you prepare for pays off more than one you leave to chance. Each rider can peak for up to two races a season, and the target race must be in your division's calendar.

peakTraining.a: When you set a peak, the planner suggests a build week and a taper week for the lead-up. Accept one from the rider drawer and it writes that rhythm to the rider's weekly training. Training the right focus for the race profile, staying consistent, and reaching the taper rested all raise how much of the peak you realize. A peak locks three days before its window starts, so plan ahead.

overlappingRaces.q: How do overlapping races and squad distribution work?
overlappingRaces.a: Races overlap when their stages fall on the same in-game race-day, and a rider can only start one race per in-game day, so spreading your squad across overlapping races is a strategic choice. Several races can share the same calendar date without conflicting when they sit on different in-game days. Each race shows its race day (for example Race day 11), so two races on the same afternoon are clearly different game days; reusing a rider across two race days is allowed, but he carries fatigue into the later race, so it is a trade-off. The squad distribution view on the Races page (Season & Results -> Races) shows your overlapping races side by side. Add riders to a race from your available pool; a rider you put in one race is locked out of the other races that overlap it, but remove him and he is immediately available again for another race. Your edits are kept locally until you press Save changes (a banner warns you if you try to leave with unsaved edits). You can save a partial line-up too: any slots you leave open are filled automatically from your available riders when the race runs. If you do not want to enter a race, withdraw from it and you simply do not start. The assistant fills your line-ups automatically as a starting point, and you can have it fill them again: choose Fill gaps to keep the picks you set by hand, or Rebuild all to replace every pick with the assistant's suggestion. Once a race has started, its line-up is final and can no longer be changed.

teamStrategyFaq.q: How do I make the assistant pick my best riders for big races?
teamStrategyFaq.a: Open Team strategy from the squad board. Add your best riders to the A-chain and mark the races that matter most as targets, and the assistant then prioritizes your A-chain for those races. You can also rank captains 1/2/3 per terrain and give riders a fixed role. Use Preview changes to see exactly how it affects your lineups, then Save and Regenerate suggestions. Picks you set by hand are never overwritten.

relaunchTeamMoney.q: What happened to my team and money in the Season 1 relaunch?
relaunchTeamMoney.a: At the relaunch everything starts over. Your previous squad, balance and transfers do not carry into the new Season 1. You receive a fresh {initialSquad}-rider squad and a starting budget of {startingBalance} CZ$, the same as every other manager.

relaunchRiderNames.q: Why are the rider names different after the relaunch?
relaunchRiderNames.a: Cycling Zone now runs on its own world of fictional riders with their own values and its own race engine, so the game no longer depends on external real-world data.

relaunchFounderBadge.a: A permanent mark on your profile that recognises you played during the beta, before the Season 1 relaunch.

relaunchNextSeasonBuys.q: I bought riders for next season before the relaunch. What happened to them?
relaunchNextSeasonBuys.a: The earlier two-season plan changed. The relaunch resets all teams, so riders bought for a future season do not carry over. Everyone restarts equally with a fresh squad.

balanceVisibility.a: No. Your balance is only visible to you.

ridersOffline.q: What happens to my riders if I don't log in?
ridersOffline.a: Your riders stay on your team. Cycling Zone does not punish you for skipping a day, so play at your own pace.

passwordReset.q: How do I reset my password?
passwordReset.a: From the login page you can click 'Forgot password?', enter your email and follow the reset link in the email. The link opens a dedicated reset page where you pick a new password.

riderTransferTiming.q: When does a rider switch teams after a transfer?
riderTransferTiming.a: The deal goes through immediately after both parties confirm. Trading is always open, so there is no waiting for a transfer window.

cancelParkedTransfer.q: Can I cancel a transfer after both parties accept?
cancelParkedTransfer.a: No. Once both parties have accepted, the deal is locked and completes right away. The system re-checks ownership, balance and squad limits just before it goes through.

aiDirectOffers.q: Can I buy AI riders with a direct offer?
aiDirectOffers.a: No. AI riders cannot receive direct transfer or swap offers. They must be bought via auction.

auctionExpiry.a: An auction runs for 1 active hour. Night hours don't count: the active window is 08:00 to 24:00 every day. Example: an auction started at 19:40 ends the same evening at 20:40. An auction started at 23:30 ends the next morning at 08:30.

flashAuction.a: Flash Auctions are a special auction type that's only available during Deadline Day. They run for exactly 30 minutes from the start time, regardless of active window. You find the option on the rider's stats page when Deadline Day is active.

minBidAmount.q: How much do I need to bid above the current price?
minBidAmount.a: Your bid must be at least 1 CZ$ above the current price. If no one has bid yet, you can match the asking price. Active auctions where you lead count as reserved balance and potential squad slot at the same time.

bidVisibility.q: Can I see who has bid on my rider?
bidVisibility.a: No. Transfer negotiations are private: only buyer and seller can see them. You get an anonymous notification when someone views your rider.

secretAchievementsFaq.a: Secret achievements show as locked and are only revealed when you unlock them. They are triggered by unexpected actions in the game.

onlineStatusFaq.a: A green dot next to a manager's name means they've been active within the last 5 minutes. You can see this everywhere in the game.

watchlistCounterFaq.q: What does the watchlist counter on a rider page mean?
watchlistCounterFaq.a: It shows how many managers have that rider on their watchlist. You can't see who, only the total count.

scoutVisibilityFaq.q: Can other managers see that I scouted a rider?
scoutVisibilityFaq.a: The rider's owner can. On their own rider's profile, the Interest tab lists which teams have used scout slots on him, how many, and when. Everyone else only sees anonymous totals (how many rivals scouted him), never which teams.

developmentProjectionFaq.q: What is the projection on the Development tab?
developmentProjectionFaq.a: It is a forecast of where a rider is heading: a dashed line from his current type rating toward a shaded ceiling band, with a rough estimate of how many seasons and at what age he reaches it. It is always a range, never an exact number, because nobody knows a rider's exact ceiling. The band is deliberately fuzzy so it cannot be reverse-engineered into his hidden potential, and it stays hidden for rival riders you have not scouted.

watchlistSaleNotificationFaq.q: Do I get notified when a rider on my watchlist is listed for sale?
watchlistSaleNotificationFaq.a: Yes. If a rider you're tracking is listed on the transfer list or put up for auction, you automatically get a notification in the inbox with price and type. You don't need to actively check the market for your favourites.

riderSalaryView.q: How do I see a rider's salary?
riderSalaryView.a: Salary is a visible column in the Rider Database and watchlist, and can be sorted and filtered on. You can filter on a salary range under 'Salary CZ$ (min-max)' in the filter panel. The size of the salary depends on the rider's market value, which also includes the average of the rider's recent season prize money.

riderDevelopment.q: Where can I see whether a rider is developing?
riderDevelopment.a: Open the rider's stats page and pick the 'Development' tab. It shows a selectable graph for each stat and the rider's recorded development over time.

trainingFocusFaq.q: How do I train a rider?
trainingFocusFaq.a: Open one of your own riders and set a training focus under 'Training focus' on the profile: pick a direction and an intensity. The trained abilities develop faster toward their ceiling at the next season change. You have 3 focus slots per season. Higher intensity trains faster but risks a setback; changing an existing focus costs nothing, and training is free.

salaryShortfall.q: What happens if I can't pay the salaries?
salaryShortfall.a: Salaries are deducted at season start AFTER sponsor money is paid out. If your balance + sponsor minus loan interest doesn't cover the salaries, the system automatically takes an emergency loan for the shortfall. Interest on the emergency loan is added at the next season's start.

debtCeiling.q: What is the debt ceiling, and when does it apply?
debtCeiling.a: Each division has a maximum debt amount: Division 1: 1,200,000 CZ$, Division 2: 900,000 CZ$, Division 3: 600,000 CZ$. New short-term and long-term loans are rejected if the loan plus its fee would push your total debt over the ceiling. The fee counts towards the cap, so the max you can borrow is a bit less than the room under the cap. The loan form on the Finances page shows your current max. Automatic emergency loans can exceed the ceiling, but you get a warning and must cut costs. Loan interest is added at season start on top of existing debt (moved from season end in v3.78).

prizeMoneyFaq.q: What is prize money, and when is it paid out?
prizeMoneyFaq.a: Prize money is a direct CZ$ reward for results your riders achieve in races. The formula is: points for the placing × {prizePerPoint} CZ$. The money is paid automatically to your balance when admin finalises results and shows as 'prize' in your finance log. Only races with a race class (Grand Tour, WorldTour, ProSeries etc.) generate prize money. Races without a race class give 0.

divisionBonusFaq.q: What is the division bonus, and when is it paid out?
divisionBonusFaq.a: The division bonus is a one-time bonus paid at season end based on your team's final placing in your division. Division 1: 300,000 / 200,000 / 100,000 / 50,000 CZ$ for places 1-4. Division 2: 150,000 / 100,000 / 50,000 / 25,000 CZ$ for places 1-4. Division 3: 75,000 / 50,000 / 25,000 CZ$ for places 1-3. The bonus is booked as 'bonus' in your finance log.

sponsorPayoutTiming.q: When is sponsor money paid out?
sponsorPayoutTiming.a: Sponsor money is paid at season start. One exception at the very beginning: if you start your first season with your full founding budget, you do not also receive a season-1 sponsor payout on top (that would be double income at the start). From season 2 onwards sponsor is always paid. Season 1 is an intro season with a division-scaled base (D1 600,000 · D2 400,000 · D3 340,000 CZ$) before any board or pullout effects. From season 2 a performance share of 0-150,000 CZ$ is added on top of that same division base, based on the previous season's points and placing in your division. The amount is then multiplied by the board's budget modifier and any sponsor pullout. This means strong results can lift the sponsorship, while a bottom placing still keeps the full division base before modifier.

sponsorNegotiation.q: How do I negotiate my sponsor, and what do the offers mean?
sponsorNegotiation.a: At the start of a new season you choose your sponsor on the Board page, where three offers are on the table. The offers are shaped by your club's reputation: this is built from your division and your results over recent seasons, so a strong, established club is offered larger deals than a fresh or low-standing one. Each offer trades two income parts against a contract length of 1 to 3 seasons. The guaranteed base is paid every season no matter how much you race, while the per-race-day rate pays a fixed amount for every race day your team competes in, credited as races finalize. A steady backer leans on the guaranteed base, a race-day backer pays more per race day (best if you race the full calendar), and a long-term partner locks the same terms in for longer so they don't reset each season. Once you sign, the deal runs for its full length and is shown on the Finance page under the Sponsor tab; you negotiate again when it expires.

seasonFinanceReport.q: What does the season finance report show, and where do I find it?
seasonFinanceReport.a: The season finance report is a dedicated page per season that shows your cashflow for the chosen season: the hero card at the top sums total income + spend + net result, two donut charts split income and spending by category (sponsor, prize money, auction buys, salary etc.), the top-3 lists highlight your biggest transactions in each direction, and the loan portfolio table shows active loans with remaining debt + next season's expected interest. The report is private: you only see your own, and admins see one team at a time via a team dropdown. You find the report under the History tab on the Finances page, where a season picker lets you switch between your seasons; the season snapshot page (/seasons) also links straight to it. The sponsor modifier curve and direct comparison with the previous season are first available from season 2, once there's at least one closed season to compare against.

forecastCalculation.q: How is the forecast for next season on the Finances page computed?
forecastCalculation.a: The forecast shows expected cashflow for the entire next season as the sum of: (1) sponsor = season's sponsor base × board's budget modifier × any active sponsor pullout factor. Both season 1 and season 2+ use a division-scaled base (D1 600,000 · D2 400,000 · D3 340,000 CZ$); from season 2 up to 150,000 CZ$ variable sponsor is added on top, from the previous season's points/rank. (2) prize money = the prize part of each rider's value (a rolling average of their prize money over the last 1-3 completed seasons), (3) salary = the sum of your riders' salaries, (4) loan interest = the interest on every active loan, calculated on the debt still outstanding, and (5) loan fees for loaned riders extending into next season (offset by any fees you receive as lender). The risk tier badge is green if net ≥ +50K and debt < 50% of ceiling, yellow if net is between -50K and +50K or debt is between 50-80% of ceiling, and red if net < -50K, debt > 80% of ceiling, or if your deficit hits the debt ceiling within 2 seasons. The range (±20% on the prize estimate) reflects that prize money is the most variable input. The forecast is not a contract; numbers can change if you sell riders, take loans, score more points or negotiate a new board plan.

standingsUpdate.q: When are the season standings updated?
standingsUpdate.a: Season standings update when admin finalises race results. For manager-submitted files this happens at approval, while admin-imported results update standings immediately through the same backend path.

adminFinishWithPending.q: Can admin finish the season while there are pending race approvals?
adminFinishWithPending.a: No. Pending race results must first be approved or rejected before season completion can run.

boardSatisfaction.a: The board evaluates you on the agreed targets with a gradual model where results weigh the most, but economy, identity and ranking also count. Satisfaction is alive during the season: after every race weekend it moves towards what a full season evaluation of your current standing would give, at most 5 points per weekend, so a single bad weekend can never alone trigger hard consequences. The sponsor modifier follows satisfaction live, while hard consequence layers are only evaluated at the mid-season and season-end checkpoints. New plans get scaled by your division, your squad depth and the team's derived sporting profile, so the demands fit reality better. A near-miss or strong progress can therefore still give an acceptable overall rating. A clear national core also adds a bit of identity value to the board's read of the team, while big-profile riders add some sponsor/prestige but also raise expectations. High satisfaction gives bonus sponsorship, while low satisfaction reduces the sponsor payout and can lead to stricter demands in the next plan. You cannot be fired by the board in Cycling Zone.

boardNegotiation.q: Can I negotiate the board's demands?
boardNegotiation.a: Yes. When you open a new board negotiation the system generates the demands on the server based on division, squad size, U25 share, national core, star profile and the team's current sporting profile, and you can negotiate each target once. An approved compromise lowers the demand slightly and typically halves the penalty for missing it. Balanced teams with a clear national core can also get a national identity target as part of the plan. Once the plan is active, you can also send one board request per season from the Board page to ask for a strategic adjustment. The reply can be approved, partial, rejected or approved with a tradeoff, and direct switches between youth and star focus are often done gradually via a balanced intermediate station instead of an immediate hard switch. The Board page now also shows the concrete focus and target changes from the latest request, so you can see exactly what the board changed.

directYouthToStarSwitch.q: Can I switch directly between youth focus and star focus?
directYouthToStarSwitch.a: Usually only partially. The board can accept a new direction, but if the team's current identity points clearly towards youth or big-profile riders, the switch is often made as a tradeoff via a more balanced plan first. This makes requests more predictable and adds friction to identity shifts, so the team's DNA doesn't flip overnight.

boardCurrentAssessment.q: Where do I see the board's current assessment?
boardCurrentAssessment.a: The Dashboard shows a short board outlook with status and category scores, while the Board page shows the more detailed assessment. The Board page also shows the board's read of the team with specialisation, U25 share, national core, star profile and squad status, now with country name and flag on the national core. The Board page now also explains more clearly why the board reacts, by showing which categories drive the assessment, what's holding it back, and which signals from history, identity or profiles play in. The two places read the same board data from the backend, so they share the same truth.

boardProfileRiders.q: Who are my team's profile riders?
boardProfileRiders.a: The Board page now names them. Under the star profile tile, the board's read of the team lists each rider it counts as a star, with a star score. A rider qualifies as a star when popularity plus UCI points clear the star threshold. Popularity weighs more than results (roughly 70/30), so a well-known rider can be a star even without big point hauls, while a strong but anonymous worker usually is not. The list is your current squad, so it changes as you sign or sell riders. If no rider clears the threshold yet, the card says so instead of listing names.

season1BaselineFaq.q: Is the board available in season 1?
season1BaselineFaq.a: Yes. The board is unlocked from the start of season 1, so you can pick your Club DNA and negotiate your 5-year, 3-year and 1-year plans right away. Until you sign your first plan the sponsor modifier stays at 1.0×, so the season-1 sponsor is neutral while you set up. The board also *reads* your team (which nation dominates, how large the U25 share is, which specialisation the team points towards, and how visible your stars are) and saves this 'identity snapshot' on your team permanently as the basis for your 5-year plan suggestions.

buildOnCoreBadge.q: What does the 'Builds on your core' badge mean on 5-year targets?
buildOnCoreBadge.a: It's identity feeding. When the board proposes 5-year targets in season 2, relevant targets are annotated with a small badge explaining why that target exists. Example: 'Builds on your FR core (5/8 riders)' when your season 1 team had five French riders out of eight. Click the badge for the full explanation. The badge shows the frozen season 1 observation, i.e. even if you later switch riders out, the board remembers which signal originally drove the target.

forgotToNegotiatePlan.q: What happens if I forget to negotiate my plan?
forgotToNegotiatePlan.a: The board takes over. If you haven't signed a new plan before race day 5 is passed, the board automatically signs a plan with default focus derived from your team's identity (U25-heavy → youth development, star profile → star signing, otherwise balanced) and standard targets. You first get an info reminder at race day 2 ('The board is waiting for your N-year plan'), then an 'Action required' notification at race day 4 ('Last chance'), and after that it's the board's pick. You can still request changes via a board request once the plan is running. If you check the Board page often, you'll also see a live countdown banner with race days remaining.

boardMembersFaq.q: Who are the members of my board?
boardMembersFaq.a: The board has 5 named members from a pool of 9 archetypes: The Sponsor Pleaser (guards sponsor relationships), The Traditionalist (the club's heritage), The Talent Scout (long-term youth), The Result Hunter (wants to win now), The Pragmatist (balance), The Youth Idealist (future over now), The Nationalist Purist (home colours), The Classics Purist (monuments), The GC Lover (Tour). Three members are matched to the team's identity at end of season 1, two are chosen as wildcards that bring contrast, but never direct contradiction (a debt-aversion purist isn't paired with a risk taker). The avatar grid on the Board page shows who's who; the one with ★ is the chairman.

boardChairman.q: What does the board chairman do?
boardChairman.a: The chairman is the member with the highest alignment to your team. He speaks when in doubt: if the board's assessment doesn't have a clear dominant category, it's the chairman's voice you hear. He's also the one who gets replaced if the board loses patience: 2× consecutive plan expiry below 30% satisfaction → a new chairman is picked from the 4 unassigned archetypes. You get a 'The board has picked a new chairman' notification when it happens.

memberReactions.q: Why do different board members react to different targets?
memberReactions.a: Each archetype 'owns' certain categories: The Sponsor Pleaser owns economy targets, The Result Hunter owns results, The Traditionalist/Nationalist Purist owns identity, The Pragmatist owns ranking. When you click the 'X reacts' button on a target, a quote shows from the member whose category the target falls under. The quotes are hand-written per archetype and per situation (proposal, met, missed), so The Sponsor Pleaser sounds worried about a softening debt target, and The Result Hunter sounds excited about a win.

starSigningGoal.q: What does the new 'star rider' target in star_signing plans mean?
starSigningGoal.a: Star_signing focus now gets a 5th target: 'At least 1 star rider (popularity ≥75)'. The board expects that a plan to chase results also manifests as at least one rider with a high profile, whether you buy an existing star on the market or develop one internally. Checked at evaluation time, so it's your current squad that counts. You can negotiate the target, but it's already the minimum (target=1).

u25StatGain.q: What is 'U25 stat gain' in youth plans?
u25StatGain.a: Youth_development focus now gets a 5th target: 'Average U25 stat gain ≥3 stat points/season'. It measures whether your U25 riders actually develop, not just that they're on the team. Plan-start baseline is snapshotted automatically at season end, and the board compares against the current average. A development programme that just moves young riders in but doesn't develop them doesn't meet the target. The board expects to see actual growth.

balancedRelativeRank.q: What is 'finish ahead of X other managers' in balanced plans?
balancedRelativeRank.a: Balanced focus now gets a 5th target: 'Finish ahead of at least 3 other managers in the division'. It's not about absolute ranking, but relative: you need to beat over half in your division. Scales automatically when more managers join: if there are 5 human managers in your division and you're rank 1, you've beaten 4. If you're rank 4, you've only beaten 1 → target not met. It's read from your current rank in the division against the number of human managers in it.

boardUnsatisfiedConsequences.q: What happens when the board is very unhappy, can I be fired?
boardUnsatisfiedConsequences.a: You cannot be fired. Instead the board reacts gradually through 6 layers the lower satisfaction goes: at <40% it imposes a salary cap (you can't grow the team's total salary, sell a rider first). At <30% comes a signing restriction: buys above 300K CZ$ are blocked. At <15% the board force-lists one rider on the market: it picks your lowest-value rider and lists him at his market value. Your stars are protected here, any rider with popularity 70 or higher, or a value of 8,000,000 CZ$ or more, is left alone. At <10% a main sponsor pulls out: sponsor income is reduced by 10% for the next season. All active consequences show in the 'Active consequences' panel on the Board page, and layers 4-5 also send 'Action required' notifications. Satisfaction that rises again automatically expires layers 2-3.

bonusOffer.q: What is the bonus offer I just got from the board?
bonusOffer.a: When satisfaction is above 75% AND at least 75% of your plan targets are met, the board can offer you +200K CZ$ to the balance against one extra target. If your focus is star_signing, the target will be 'Sign 1 star (popularity ≥75)'; otherwise it will be 'Top-3 in at least 1 monument'. You can accept or decline. Accept and the budget is credited immediately via a bonus finance tx, and the target is added to your 1-year plan and evaluated at season end. The board only offers one bonus per season, so if you decline, the chance comes again next season only if the criteria are still met.

boardSatisfactionArrow.q: What is the arrow/prognosis under my satisfaction number?
boardSatisfactionArrow.a: It shows where satisfaction is heading, not just where it is. Every race weekend the board runs a full evaluation of your current season and calculates a target based on your season-start satisfaction plus that evaluation. The live number then moves towards the target by at most 5 points per weekend (the same clamp described under 'What is board satisfaction?'), so you can see 'on its way to 71%' before it actually gets there. If the number is already at target, you'll see 'already at target' instead.

boardChairmanWarning.q: What does the red warning about the chairman mean?
boardChairmanWarning.a: It appears when your team already has one consecutive plan expiry below 30% satisfaction on record. One more disappointing plan expiry in a row and the board replaces the chairman with a new archetype, per the rule described under 'What does the board chairman do?'. The warning disappears again once a plan renews with satisfaction above the threshold, resetting the counter.

bonusOfferDistance.q: How do I know how close I am to a bonus offer?
bonusOfferDistance.a: Each plan card shows your distance to the bonus offer eligibility (satisfaction above 75% AND at least 75% of targets met, see 'What is the bonus offer I just got from the board?'). If satisfaction is still too low, you'll see how many points are missing; once satisfaction clears the bar, you'll see how many more targets you need to hit ('2 goals from a bonus offer'). Once both conditions are met it just says you're eligible.

boardMemberWeights.q: What does a board member weigh when I open their card?
boardMemberWeights.a: Click a member in the avatar grid to open their card.
